$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `doc_mount_files` repo — the NARROWEST
mount-index sibling-DB repo of Phase 2 (a required sha256 TEXT, one REAL-int
column, two enum TEXT columns; no JSON, no boolean, no nullable columns). One
committed file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-doc-mount-files-fixture.ts + doc-mount-files-tier2.ts)
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness doc_mount_files tier-2 test).
SIBLING DB: in v4 this table lives in quilltap-mount-index.db (NOT the main
DB). The builder/oracle point SQLITE_MOUNT_INDEX_PATH at the fixture and read
it back through getRawMountIndexDatabase(); the Rust Writer simply opens that
file by path. Mirrors the doc_mount_points machinery exactly.
FRESH-FIXTURE NOTE: v4's getCollection() runs generateDDL (which emits exactly
the 7 current schema columns) plus a non-UNIQUE sha256 lookup index
(idx_doc_mount_files_sha256). The index accelerates reads only — it changes no
persisted row bytes, so it is harmless for the state diff. There are NO runtime
ALTER-TABLE migrations on this repo. The fixture has EXACTLY the 7 schema cols.
ALL FIELDS SET EXPLICITLY on every create so v4's source.default('filesystem')
never fires and both sides insert identical bytes.
AFFINITY: fileSizeBytes is z.number().int().min(0) — min only, NO max → REAL by
v4's mapToSQLiteType (bound f64). The canonical dump collapses integer-valued
REAL back to a JSON integer, so 0/5242880/etc. match v4.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one every other
tier-2 fixture uses. It is NOT the real Friday pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.
BANKED across the corpus: fileSizeBytes both 0 and >0; both enum columns
exercised across multiple variants (pdf/markdown/blob; filesystem/database).

ops.comment: Add a markdown content row; all fields explicit (no Zod default fires). fileSizeBytes>0, source filesystem.
Re-fingerprint seed row 2: new sha256, bump fileSizeBytes 0->non-zero, change fileType blob->docx and source database->filesystem. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.
Remove seed row 1 (the pdf content row).
